arsenal.title: The Gold Standard
arsenal.fact: The only nation to win 5 FIFA World Cups (1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, 2002) and the only country to play in every single tournament.

arsenal.title: Jogo Bonito Legacy
arsenal.fact: Synonymous with beautiful, expressive football. Produced the most Ballon d'Or winners and legendary forwards (Pelé, Ronaldo Nazário, Ronaldinho, Neymar).

arsenal.title: Global Talent Pool
arsenal.fact: The world's largest exporter of professional football players, filling top leagues across Europe and Asia with creative sparks.

arsenal.title: Pelé's Hegemony
arsenal.fact: Home of Pelé, the only player to win 3 World Cups, who scored 1281 goals in his career and remains the ultimate global icon.

counterattacks.claim: Brazil has not won a World Cup since 2002. For over 20 years, European nations and Argentina have dominated, while Brazil suffered humiliating defeats (like the 7-1 to Germany).
counterattacks.defenseUpgrade: Acknowledge the post-2002 drought as a transition, but highlight that Brazil's historical 5 stars remain an unmatched peak, and they continue to produce the world's most exciting talent (Vini Jr, Rodrygo).

counterattacks.claim: The 'Jogo Bonito' is dead in the modern tactical, physical era. Brazilian flair has been replaced by structured European systems, and Argentina has adapted far better.
counterattacks.defenseUpgrade: Argue that Brazilian flair is not dead; it has evolved. Players like Vinicius Jr. combine Jogo Bonito dribbling with modern European tactical disciplines, winning UCL finals.

counterattacks.claim: Pelé played in an era with weak tactical setups and offside rules. Messi's 15+ years of European dominance at the absolute highest level eclipses Pelé's career.
counterattacks.defenseUpgrade: Remind that Pelé played against the most brutal, unprotected tackles in football history, won his first World Cup at 17, and proved his genius against top-tier European teams in friendlies and Intercontinental Cups.

strengths: Excellent defense of Brazil's unmatched 5 World Cup stars.
Great leverage of the Pelé legacy and Jogo Bonito aesthetic.
weaknesses: Allowed the 7-1 Germany defeat and recent World Cup drought to weaken your position.
Struggled to answer Argentina's recent Copa América and World Cup triumphs.
missedOpportunities: Could have highlighted Brazil's positive overall head-to-head record in Copa América history.
Should have pointed out how many Ballon d'Ors Brazilian players have won since 1990 compared to Argentina (excluding Messi).
coachFeedback.upgradeSuggestion: Pivot the discussion from recent years to global impact. Define Brazil not as a team that wins occasionally, but as the very soul and identity of global football. Re-emphasize that Brazil's 5 stars is the ultimate trophy metric.

round1.comment: Strong appeal to historical pedigree and beautiful football, but vulnerable on recent trophy drought.

round2.comment: Brilliant rebuttal. Framing Brazil as the soul of football and highlighting modern world-beaters like Vinicius Jr. recaptured the panel's score.
